phaseA.question: How much energy do you actually have for this path, right now?
phaseA.hint: Not how much you wish you had. Today's truth.

phaseA.question: How much meditation experience do you bring?
phaseA.hint: This tells the recommendation engine where to start you.

options.label: Never sat
options.desc: I have never really meditated. I need the basics, clearly given.

options.label: A little
options.desc: Apps, occasional sits, maybe a course years ago. Not consistent.

options.label: Regular practice
options.desc: I sit most days. The basics are known. Consistency is there.

options.label: Long practice
options.desc: Years of practice, retreats done, a teacher or lineage behind me.

phaseA.question: What do you hope this quest helps with?
phaseA.hint: Pick up to two. Guides the recommendation.

options.label: Build a daily practice
options.desc: The thread unbroken — the first thing the Buddha asked of his disciples.

options.label: Deepen what I already have
options.desc: The sit is there; I want the next step.

options.label: Understand the dhamma
options.desc: The suttas, the map of the path, how the pieces fit.

options.label: Work with a specific hindrance
options.desc: One of the five is particularly heavy right now.

phaseA.question: If you had to name the strongest pull in your mind lately, which would it be?
phaseA.hint: Tap the one closest to the truth. The others can be named separately.

options.label: Sensual desire
options.desc: The pull of pleasure — food, screens, attention, comfort.

options.label: Ill will
options.desc: Irritation, resentment, quiet judgment, the silent "no".

options.label: Sloth & torpor
options.desc: Heaviness, dullness, the mind closing rather than opening.

options.label: Restlessness
options.desc: The inability to settle. The next tab. The need to move.

options.label: Doubt
options.desc: Is this working? Am I doing it right? What's the point?

phaseB_beginner.question: Optional — what has stopped a daily practice from sticking before?
phaseB_beginner.hint: You can skip this. One or two lines — honest, not polished. If anything comes to mind, it helps the engine set the bar realistically.
phaseB_beginner.placeholder: e.g. "I forget in the morning" or "10 minutes always feels too long"

phaseB_beginner.question: How many minutes could you sit today, honestly, without bargaining?
phaseB_beginner.hint: The number you know you can actually keep, not the number you wish for.

phaseB_beginner.question: How would you like to sit?
phaseB_beginner.hint: Comfort matters more than form. The Buddha never said the posture makes the meditation — he sat on grass. Whatever lets you stay present is right.

options.label: A chair
options.desc: Feet flat on the ground, spine long, hands on thighs. Completely honorable.

options.label: A cushion on the floor
options.desc: Cross-legged or kneeling. Knees below hips if possible.

options.label: A meditation bench (seiza)
options.desc: Kneeling with the bench taking the weight off the ankles.

options.label: Lying down, for now
options.desc: Honest choice if the body needs it. Stay alert — don't fall asleep if you can help it.

options.label: Not sure yet
options.desc: I'll try and see what feels right.

phaseB_beginner.question: When can you most realistically sit?
phaseB_beginner.hint: The hour you can actually keep, not the ideal one. The classical advice is first thing in the morning, but the best time is the one you will not skip.

options.label: First thing in the morning
options.desc: Before anything else. The classical advice.

options.label: Mid-morning
options.desc: After the first rush of the day has settled.

options.label: Lunch or midday
options.desc: A pause in the middle of the day.

options.label: Evening, before bed
options.desc: To close the day. Helps sleep in many practitioners.

options.label: Varies day to day
options.desc: Life is like that right now. The app will adapt.

phaseB_beginner.question: Optional — any physical concerns to be aware of? Back, knees, pain, sleep?
phaseB_beginner.hint: You can skip this. The engine will soften advice around anything you mention — no posture suggestion that makes an existing pain worse.
phaseB_beginner.placeholder: e.g. "bad knees, can't sit cross-legged" or "chronic back pain"

phaseB_beginner.question: Optional — anything about starting a meditation practice that makes you nervous or unsure?
phaseB_beginner.hint: You can skip this. Nothing is too small or too much. The reassurance on your first screens will be shaped by what you name here.
phaseB_beginner.placeholder: e.g. "I can't stop thinking" or "I've tried and failed before"

phaseB_experienced.question: What is your primary tradition or lineage?
phaseB_experienced.hint: Tells the engine which language and framing will feel native.

options.label: Goenka / Vipassana
options.desc: 10-day courses, body-scanning, the body as the field of sensation.

options.label: Theravāda (other)
options.desc: Forest tradition, Mahasi, Pa Auk, Ajahn Chah lineage, etc.

options.label: Mahāyāna / Tibetan
options.desc: Zen, Pure Land, Tibetan Buddhism, Chinese Buddhism.

options.label: Secular mindfulness
options.desc: MBSR, mindfulness-based therapy, insight without the metaphysics.

options.label: Mixed / no one tradition
options.desc: Multiple streams, or following what works pragmatically.

options.label: Other
options.desc: Yoga, Advaita, Sufi, contemplative Christianity, etc.

phaseB_experienced.question: Teachers or books that have shaped your practice?
phaseB_experienced.hint: Tap all that apply. Helps the engine match tone and framing. Nothing here is required — "mixed / none listed" is a perfectly honest answer.

options.label: The Pali suttas directly (Bodhi, Ñāṇamoli, Access to Insight translations)
Other teacher(s) — I will name them if the engine asks
No specific teacher or book — I have drawn on many

phaseB_experienced.question: Typical sit length right now, in minutes?
phaseB_experienced.hint: The actual average, not the aspirational.

phaseB_experienced.question: Where is the edge of your practice right now?
phaseB_experienced.hint: What you are actively working on, or what you would work on if you had guidance.

options.label: Concentration / samatha
options.desc: Access concentration, jhāna factors, steady one-pointedness.

options.label: Insight / vipassanā
options.desc: Seeing the three characteristics directly — anicca, dukkha, anattā.

options.label: Metta / brahmavihāras
options.desc: The heart practices — kindness, compassion, sympathetic joy, equanimity.

options.label: Off-cushion integration
options.desc: Bringing sati into speech, work, relationships, the daily tangle.

options.label: A specific hindrance
options.desc: One of the five is the real work right now.

options.label: Subtle defilements
options.desc: Conceit, restlessness about fine things, the last craving for becoming.

phaseB_experienced.question: What do you want from this tool that you don't already have?
phaseB_experienced.hint: Pick all that apply. Honest answers shape what the engine surfaces first — habits, reflections, suttas, or structure. Picking nothing is fine too.

options.label: Continuity — especially when I travel or life is disrupted
A structured reading plan for the suttas
Help building supporting habits beyond the sit (speech, reflection, generosity)
Honest pattern-level feedback over weeks and months, not just a streak counter
Specific help with one hindrance that keeps resurfacing
A way to bring sati off the cushion into daily life
A sense of companionship on the path (even if only a simulated one for now)
I'm curious — not sure yet what I want
Something else — I'll watch and see

phaseB_experienced.question: Which of these are the Four Noble Truths?
phaseB_experienced.hint: Not a test — no scoring, no wrong-answer penalty. The engine uses what you pick (and what you miss) to decide whether foundation material belongs in your first weeks or not.

options.label: The truth of dukkha — suffering, unsatisfactoriness
The path to cessation — the noble eightfold path
The law of karma and its fruits
Rebirth in the realms of saṃsāra
The cultivation of compassion for all beings

phaseB_experienced.question: Which of these belong to the Noble Eightfold Path?
phaseB_experienced.hint: Select all that belong. The ones you miss tell the engine where the foundation may be thin — regardless of how long you've been sitting.

options.label: Right faith (distractor — saddhā is a faculty, not a path factor)
Right compassion (distractor — karuṇā is a brahmavihāra)

phaseB_experienced.question: Where, if anywhere, does your practice feel stuck right now?
phaseB_experienced.hint: The honest answer tells the engine what to surface first. No answer makes you less of a practitioner.

options.label: Plateau — nothing changes
options.desc: Sitting steadily but no movement in depth or insight.

options.label: Dryness — practice has lost warmth
options.desc: The sit is mechanical. The heart is absent. Metta may be thin.

options.label: Strong on the cushion, weak off it
options.desc: Hours of sitting don't translate to daily life. Sati fails when stress rises.

options.label: Sitting hard but missing something
options.desc: Classical Goenka / intensive-retreat pattern. The foundation of sīla or view may be thin.

options.label: Something dark has opened
options.desc: Dark night, difficult territory. A real teacher is what this needs.

options.label: Doubt about the path itself
options.desc: The method, the tradition, or the whole project.

options.label: Not stuck — practice is alive
options.desc: Moving, learning, integrating. Good.

phaseB_experienced.question: Optional — anything unclear or unresolved about the path for you right now?
phaseB_experienced.hint: You can skip this. Anything you write — doctrinal, practical, personal — helps the engine surface the right sutta. It will not be shared anywhere.
phaseB_experienced.placeholder: e.g. "anattā — I understand it intellectually but can't see it in experience" or "I don't know why sīla matters if the mind is already settled"

phaseC.question: Pull of sensual desire right now
phaseC.example: Food cravings, scrolling, attraction, wanting pleasant sensations. The mind leaning toward what it wants.

phaseC.question: Ill will / irritation right now
phaseC.example: Anger, resentment, judgment, impatience. The mind pushing away what it does not want.

phaseC.question: Restlessness when you try to be still
phaseC.example: The body fidgets, the mind jumps, worry spirals, can't settle into a sit. Also: regret replaying.

phaseC.question: Doubt about this path
phaseC.example: Not sure the practice works, questioning the teacher/teaching, suspicion of your own motivation.

phaseC.question: Clarity of purpose for doing this
phaseC.example: Why are you doing this at all? Clear purpose supports every other factor; lost purpose undermines everything.
